name: arp-scan
title: arp-scan
description:
arp scanning and fingerprinting tool
arp-scan is a command-line tool that uses the ARP protocol to discover and
fingerprint IP hosts on the local network. It is available for Linux and BSD
under the GPL licence

commands.name: arp-scan
commands.description: Send ARP requests to target hosts and display responses

commands.name: get-oui
commands.description: Fetch the arp-scan OUI file from the ieee-data package

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use arp-fingerprint because arp scanning and fingerprinting tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use arp-fingerprint as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arp-fingerprint [options] target` as documented upstream for arp-fingerprint.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arp-fingerprint $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST` as documented upstream for arp-fingerprint.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arp-fingerprint -o "-N -I eth1" $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST` as documented upstream for arp-fingerprint.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair arp-fingerprint with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `arp-fingerprint --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: arp-fingerprint - Fingerprint a system using ARP
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install arp-scan (1.10.0-2). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `arp-fingerprint $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `arp-fingerprint -o "-N -I eth1" $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: arp scanning and fingerprinting tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: arp-fingerprint - Fingerprint a system using ARP.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install arp-scan (1.10.0-2)
